<think>I now have a complete and thorough picture of all 9 crates. I've read every source .rs file and every Cargo.toml. I have all the evidence I need to provide a comprehensive report.

Let me summarize my findings:

**Implementation code vs skeleton/empty:**

1. **truefix** (facade): Has a real lib.rs that re-exports from core/session/transport/config. It's a thin facade — implementation is re-exports, not skeleton/empty per se but minimal.

2. **truefix-at** (acceptance test suite): SKELETON/EMPTY — lib.rs is just a module doc comment + lint denies, no actual code. Cargo.toml has no dependencies. Explicitly "scaffolding (Stage S0)".

3. **truefix-config**: REAL implementation — `.cfg` parser with `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` sections, inheritance, `${name}` interpolation, error types, and tests.

4. **truefix-core**: FULL implementation — Field, FieldMap, Group, Message types, codec (encode/decode with BodyLength/CheckSum), typed accessors (int/decimal/char/bool/timestamp), tags, MessageFactory trait, MessageCracker trait.

5. **truefix-dict**: FULL implementation — DataDictionary model, parser for normalized dict format, runtime validation, FIXT 1.1 separation, FNV-1a hash, build.rs codegen, bundled FIX44/FIXT11/FIX50 dictionary sources.

6. **truefix-log**: FULL implementation — Log trait, ScreenLog, FileLog, CompositeLog, TracingLog, build_log factory.

7. **truefix-session**: FULL implementation — sans-IO Session state machine (logon/heartbeat/test-request/logout), sequence gap recovery, resend request/gap-fill, SequenceReset, Application trait, SessionConfig, SessionId, admin message builders, validator integration.

## Summary: crates/ exploration complete

I explored all 9 subdirectories under `/Users/jiayin/workspace/dev/dev/rust/truefix/crates/`. I listed all files, read all 9 `Cargo.toml` files, and read **every** `src/**/*.rs` source file (all 58 `.rs` files). Below is the per-crate assessment of **actual implementation vs skeleton/empty**.

### Verdict at a glance
| Crate | Status | LOC / substance |
|---|---|---|
| `truefix` (facade) | Thin real facade (re-exports only) | ~30 lines, pure `pub use` re-exports |
| `truefix-at` | **SKELETON / EMPTY** | doc comment + lints only, no deps, no code |
| `truefix-config` | **Full implementation** | `.cfg` parser, ~250 lines + tests |
| `truefix-core` | **Full implementation** | message model + SOH codec, ~12 modules |
| `truefix-dict` | **Full implementation** | runtime dict + build-time codegen + 3 bundled dicts |
| `truefix-log` | **Full implementation** | Log trait + 4 backends + factory |
| `truefix-session` | **Full implementation** | sans-IO state machine, ~600 lines |
| `truefix-store` | **Full implementation** | 5 store backends incl. SQLite |
| `truefix-transport` | **Full implementation** | tokio initiator/acceptor, ~350 lines |

### The ONLY skeleton crate: `truefix-at`
`crates/truefix-at/src/lib.rs` contains only a module-level doc comment (`//! TrueFix acceptance-test (AT) conformance suite. … This crate is scaffolding (Stage S0); functionality lands in later stages.`) and the `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` lint block. Its `Cargo.toml` has **no dependencies** and no `[features]`. There is no code, no tests, nothing beyond the scaffolding declaration.

### Detail per implemented crate

**truefix** (`src/lib.rs`): A facade crate. Re-exports `truefix_core` (`Field`, `FieldMap`, `Group`, `Message`, `MessageCracker`, `MessageFactory`, `decode`, `encode`, errors), `truefix_session` (`Application`, `Session`, `SessionConfig`, `SessionId`, `SessionState`, `Action`, `Event`, `Role`), `truefix_transport` (`connect_initiator`, `Acceptor`, `SessionHandle`), and `truefix_config`. No logic of its own.

**truefix-config** (`src/lib.rs`): QuickFIX-style `.cfg` parser. Implements `SessionSettings::parse` handling `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` sections with per-session inheritance over defaults, `#` comments + blank-line stripping, and `${name}` variable interpolation (default section first, then each merged session against itself). Typed `ConfigError` (`Malformed`, `UnresolvedVariable`). Ships 5 inline unit tests (inheritance/override, interpolation, comments, unresolved var, malformed line).

**truefix-core** (modules: `lib`, `error`, `field`, `field_map`, `factory`, `group`, `message`, `tags`, `cracker`, `codec/{mod,encode,decode}`):
- `Field` (tag + raw bytes) with typed accessors `as_str/as_int/as_decimal/as_char/as_bool/as_utc_timestamp` (picosecond→nanosecond truncation), plus constructors (`new/string/int`).
- `FieldMap` (ordered members: `Field` or repeating `Group`), `add_field/set/get/contains/add_group/group/fields`.
- `Group` builder (count tag + entries), `Message` (header/body/trailer `FieldMap`) with `encode/decode` delegating to codec.
- `codec::encode` emits canonical `8,9,35,<header>,<body>,<trailer>,10` and computes BodyLength + CheckSum (mod 256); `codec::decode` tokenizes honoring length-prefixed binary data fields (so embedded SOH doesn't corrupt parsing), verifies BeginString/BodyLength/CheckSum, routes header/trailer/body.
- `tags.rs`: standard tag constants + `is_header`/`is_trailer`/`data_field_for_length`.
- `MessageFactory` and `MessageCracker` are **contract-only traits** (skeletons marked for Stage S4 typed dispatch).
- Tests dir: `reference_vectors.rs, roundtrip.rs, field_types.rs, groups.rs, garbled.rs`.

**truefix-dict** (modules: `lib, model, parser, validate, fixt, hash`, `build.rs`, plus bundled `dict-src/normalized/{FIX44,FIXT11,FIX50}.fixdict` + README):
- `model.rs`: `DataDictionary` (version, fields, field_by_name, messages, header, trailer, hash), `FieldDef`/`FieldType` (16 types with `value_ok` format checks), `MessageDef`, `RejectReason` (codes 0-11), `ValidationError` (session vs business), `ValidationOptions` (5 toggles matching FIX `Validate*`).
- `parser.rs`: parses a normalized text format (`version`/`field`/`header`/`trailer`/`message` with `req:`/`opt:` lists), computes FNV-1a content hash.
- `validate.rs`: `DataDictionary::validate` — checks empty values, undefined/UDF(5000+)/unknown-tag, field-type format, enumeration, message-membership, and required fields; emits session-level or business-level rejects.
- `fixt.rs`: `FixtDictionaries` for FIXT 1.1 transport/application dictionary separation with per-ApplVerID resolution + DefaultApplVerID fallback.
- `hash.rs` + `build.rs`: dual-track — build-time codegen (`generated.rs` in `OUT_DIR`) of `*_DICT_HASH` constants + `*_msgs` MsgType constants; runtime asserts hash equality. Bundled normalized dict sources included via `include_str!`; `load_fix44/load_fixt11/load_fix50` helpers.
- Tests dir: `toggles.rs, rejection_layers.rs, dual_track.rs, fixt.rs, parse.rs`.

**truefix-log** (modules: `lib, screen, file, composite, tracing_log`): `Log` trait (`on_incoming/on_outgoing/on_event`), `LogConfig` enum, `build_log` factory. Backends: `ScreenLog` (stdout/stderr), `FileLog` (separate `messages.log`/`event.log`, append, Mutex-guarded), `CompositeLog` (fan-out), `TracingLog` (`tracing` facade with message/event targets). All best-effort/infallible from caller's view.
- Tests dir: `composite.rs`.

**truefix-session** (modules: `lib, config, application, session_id, state, admin, tags, time_util`):
- `state.rs`: the **sans-IO `Session` state machine** — states (`Disconnected/AwaitingLogon/LoggedOn/AwaitingLogout`), `Event` (`Connected/Received/Tick/StartLogout`), `Action` (`Send/Disconnect`). Handles logon (initiator/acceptor, ResetSeqNumFlag, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum 789, HeartBtInt negotiation), heartbeat/test-request timers, logout, **inbound sequence validation with out-of-order queueing**, ResendRequest generation on gaps, ResendRequest handling (gap-fill via SequenceReset-GapFill + PossDup/OrigSendingTime app-message resend, chunked resend), SequenceReset-GapFill/Reset processing, optional dictionary-backed inbound validation (emits Reject/BusinessMessageReject), in-memory sent-message store.
- `admin.rs`: builders for Logon/Logout/Heartbeat/TestRequest/ResendRequest/SequenceReset/Reject/reject_with_reason/business_message_reject/prepare_resend.
- `application.rs`: async `Application` trait (on_create/on_logon/on_logout/to_admin/from_admin/to_app/from_app, no-op defaults).
- `config.rs`: `SessionConfig` (begin_string, comp IDs, heartbeat_interval, role, reset flags, resend chunk size, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum toggle, latency checks) + `Role`.
- `session_id.rs`, `time_util.rs` (UTC timestamp formatting), `tags.rs`.
- Tests dir: `validation_hook.rs, state_machine.rs, recovery.rs`.

**truefix-store** (modules: `lib, memory, file, noop, sql`): async `MessageStore` trait (next_sender_seq/next_target_seq/setters/save/get[range]/reset) + `StoreConfig` + `build_store` factory. Backends: `MemoryStore` (BTreeMap under Mutex, shared `State`), `FileStore` (durable; `seqnums` text file + `body` append log of `seq(8 LE)|len(4 LE)|bytes`, replays on open, tolerates/reports corrupt trailing record → `was_corrupted()`), `CachedFileStore` (wraps FileStore), `NoopStore`, and `SqlStore` behind `sql` feature (sqlx/SQLite, `seqnums` + `messages` tables).
- Tests dir: `restart_resend.rs`.

**truefix-transport** (modules: `lib, framing`): tokio TCP `connect_initiator`/`connect_initiator_with` and `Acceptor` (`bind`/`bind_with`/`serve` accept loop). `framing.rs` extracts complete messages from a byte stream using BodyLength + fixed 7-byte CheckSum trailer (with inline tests). `lib.rs` `run_connection` drives the sans-IO `Session`: restores seqs from store on connect, reads/frames, dispatches `Event`s, performs `Action::Send`/`Disconnect`, routes admin vs app messages through `Application` callbacks, persists seqs to store (best-effort), logs via `Log`, 1s ticker, graceful `SessionHandle::logout()`. `Services { store, log }` opt-in. README header notes multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, TLS are deferred to later stages.
- Tests dir: `integration_logon.rs`.

### Notes on scope
- I read all `src/**/*.rs` files and all `Cargo.toml`s. I did **not** read the `tests/**/*.rs` files (only confirmed their existence), nor the bundled `.fixdict` data files / README in `truefix-dict/dict-src/normalized/`.
- Every crate except `truefix-at` carries the workspace lint `deny(clippy::unwrap_used, expect_used, panic, indexing_slicing)` in non-test builds — consistent with the "no panics" constitution noted throughout.
- All `Cargo.toml`s use `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`, workspace-inherited edition/rust-version/license/repository, and `workspace = true` dependency references.
args.next_steps: If deeper analysis is wanted, read the `tests/` directories per crate (notably `truefix-session/tests/state_machine.rs` and `recovery.rs`, `truefix-dict/tests/dual_track.rs`, `truefix-transport/tests/integration_logon.rs`, `truefix-store/tests/restart_resend.rs`) and the bundled `truefix-dict/dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict` sources. The single skeleton crate to flesh out is `truefix-at`; the `MessageFactory`/`MessageCracker` traits in `truefix-core` are also still contract-only (deferred to later stages per their doc comments).

Task completed. Output: Status: success

Re-exports `truefix_core` (`Field`, `FieldMap`, `Group`, `Message`, `MessageCracker`, `MessageFactory`, `decode`, `encode`, errors), `truefix_session` (`Application`, `Session`, `SessionConfig`, `SessionId`, `SessionState`, `Action`, `Event`, `Role`), `truefix_transport` (`connect_initiator`, `Acceptor`, `SessionHandle`), and `truefix_config`. No logic of its own.

**truefix-config** (`src/lib.rs`): QuickFIX-style `.cfg` parser. Implements `SessionSettings::parse` handling `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` sections with per-session inheritance over defaults, `#` comments + blank-line stripping, and `${name}` variable interpolation (default section first, then each merged session against itself). Typed `ConfigError` (`Malformed`, `UnresolvedVariable`). Ships 5 inline unit tests (inheritance/override, interpolation, comments, unresolved var, malformed line).

**truefix-core** (modules: `lib`, `error`, `field`, `field_map`, `factory`, `group`, `message`, `tags`, `cracker`, `codec/{mod,encode,decode}`):
- `Field` (tag + raw bytes) with typed accessors `as_str/as_int/as_decimal/as_char/as_bool/as_utc_timestamp` (picosecond→nanosecond truncation), plus constructors (`new/string/int`).
- `FieldMap` (ordered members: `Field` or repeating `Group`), `add_field/set/get/contains/add_group/group/fields`.
- `Group` builder (count tag + entries), `Message` (header/body/trailer `FieldMap`) with `encode/decode` delegating to codec.
- `codec::encode` emits canonical `8,9,35,<header>,<body>,<trailer>,10` and computes BodyLength + CheckSum (mod 256); `codec::decode` tokenizes honoring length-prefixed binary data fields (so embedded SOH doesn't corrupt parsing), verifies BeginString/BodyLength/CheckSum, routes header/trailer/body.
- `tags.rs`: standard tag constants + `is_header`/`is_trailer`/`data_field_for_length`.
- `MessageFactory` and `MessageCracker` are **contract-only traits** (skeletons marked for Stage S4 typed dispatch).
- Tests dir: `reference_vectors.rs, roundtrip.rs, field_types.rs, groups.rs, garbled.rs`.

**truefix-dict** (modules: `lib, model, parser, validate, fixt, hash`, `build.rs`, plus bundled `dict-src/normalized/{FIX44,FIXT11,FIX50}.fixdict` + README):
- `model.rs`: `DataDictionary` (version, fields, field_by_name, messages, header, trailer, hash), `FieldDef`/`FieldType` (16 types with `value_ok` format checks), `MessageDef`, `RejectReason` (codes 0-11), `ValidationError` (session vs business), `ValidationOptions` (5 toggles matching FIX `Validate*`).
- `parser.rs`: parses a normalized text format (`version`/`field`/`header`/`trailer`/`message` with `req:`/`opt:` lists), computes FNV-1a content hash.
- `validate.rs`: `DataDictionary::validate` — checks empty values, undefined/UDF(5000+)/unknown-tag, field-type format, enumeration, message-membership, and required fields; emits session-level or business-level rejects.
- `fixt.rs`: `FixtDictionaries` for FIXT 1.1 transport/application dictionary separation with per-ApplVerID resolution + DefaultApplVerID fallback.
- `hash.rs` + `build.rs`: dual-track — build-time codegen (`generated.rs` in `OUT_DIR`) of `*_DICT_HASH` constants + `*_msgs` MsgType constants; runtime asserts hash equality. Bundled normalized dict sources included via `include_str!`; `load_fix44/load_fixt11/load_fix50` helpers.
- Tests dir: `toggles.rs, rejection_layers.rs, dual_track.rs, fixt.rs, parse.rs`.

**truefix-log** (modules: `lib, screen, file, composite, tracing_log`): `Log` trait (`on_incoming/on_outgoing/on_event`), `LogConfig` enum, `build_log` factory. Backends: `ScreenLog` (stdout/stderr), `FileLog` (separate `messages.log`/`event.log`, append, Mutex-guarded), `CompositeLog` (fan-out), `TracingLog` (`tracing` facade with message/event targets). All best-effort/infallible from caller's view.
- Tests dir: `composite.rs`.

**truefix-session** (modules: `lib, config, application, session_id, state, admin, tags, time_util`):
- `state.rs`: the **sans-IO `Session` state machine** — states (`Disconnected/AwaitingLogon/LoggedOn/AwaitingLogout`), `Event` (`Connected/Received/Tick/StartLogout`), `Action` (`Send/Disconnect`). Handles logon (initiator/acceptor, ResetSeqNumFlag, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum 789, HeartBtInt negotiation), heartbeat/test-request timers, logout, **inbound sequence validation with out-of-order queueing**, ResendRequest generation on gaps, ResendRequest handling (gap-fill via SequenceReset-GapFill + PossDup/OrigSendingTime app-message resend, chunked resend), SequenceReset-GapFill/Reset processing, optional dictionary-backed inbound validation (emits Reject/BusinessMessageReject), in-memory sent-message store.
- `admin.rs`: builders for Logon/Logout/Heartbeat/TestRequest/ResendRequest/SequenceReset/Reject/reject_with_reason/business_message_reject/prepare_resend.
- `application.rs`: async `Application` trait (on_create/on_logon/on_logout/to_admin/from_admin/to_app/from_app, no-op defaults).
- `config.rs`: `SessionConfig` (begin_string, comp IDs, heartbeat_interval, role, reset flags, resend chunk size, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum toggle, latency checks) + `Role`.
- `session_id.rs`, `time_util.rs` (UTC timestamp formatting), `tags.rs`.
- Tests dir: `validation_hook.rs, state_machine.rs, recovery.rs`.

**truefix-store** (modules: `lib, memory, file, noop, sql`): async `MessageStore` trait (next_sender_seq/next_target_seq/setters/save/get[range]/reset) + `StoreConfig` + `build_store` factory. Backends: `MemoryStore` (BTreeMap under Mutex, shared `State`), `FileStore` (durable; `seqnums` text file + `body` append log of `seq(8 LE)|len(4 LE)|bytes`, replays on open, tolerates/reports corrupt trailing record → `was_corrupted()`), `CachedFileStore` (wraps FileStore), `NoopStore`, and `SqlStore` behind `sql` feature (sqlx/SQLite, `seqnums` + `messages` tables).
- Tests dir: `restart_resend.rs`.

**truefix-transport** (modules: `lib, framing`): tokio TCP `connect_initiator`/`connect_initiator_with` and `Acceptor` (`bind`/`bind_with`/`serve` accept loop). `framing.rs` extracts complete messages from a byte stream using BodyLength + fixed 7-byte CheckSum trailer (with inline tests). `lib.rs` `run_connection` drives the sans-IO `Session`: restores seqs from store on connect, reads/frames, dispatches `Event`s, performs `Action::Send`/`Disconnect`, routes admin vs app messages through `Application` callbacks, persists seqs to store (best-effort), logs via `Log`, 1s ticker, graceful `SessionHandle::logout()`. `Services { store, log }` opt-in. README header notes multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, TLS are deferred to later stages.
- Tests dir: `integration_logon.rs`.

### Notes on scope
- I read all `src/**/*.rs` files and all `Cargo.toml`s. I did **not** read the `tests/**/*.rs` files (only confirmed their existence), nor the bundled `.fixdict` data files / README in `truefix-dict/dict-src/normalized/`.
- Every crate except `truefix-at` carries the workspace lint `deny(clippy::unwrap_used, expect_used, panic, indexing_slicing)` in non-test builds — consistent with the "no panics" constitution noted throughout.
- All `Cargo.toml`s use `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`, workspace-inherited edition/rust-version/license/repository, and `workspace = true` dependency references.

Next steps:

The single skeleton crate to flesh out is `truefix-at`; the `MessageFactory`/`MessageCracker` traits in `truefix-core` are also still contract-only (deferred to later stages per their doc comments).

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group.

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- This tool defaults to non-interactive execution. Set `interactive` to `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for ordinary one-shot commands such as `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact bash command to execute as `bash -c <command>`. Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

interactive.description: Request an attached interactive shell session. Use `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for normal one-shot commands like `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. Returns the lines containing matches, along with their file paths and line numbers.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.

functionDeclarations.name: complete_task
functionDeclarations.description:
Call this tool to submit your final result and complete the task. This is the ONLY way to finish.

This tool must be called ALONE in its turn. Do not call any other tools in the same turn. If you still need another tool, do not call this yet.

Your result MUST be an informative handoff for the caller (main agent), not a one-line summary.

Requirements for result:
- Use 2+ short paragraphs or a short markdown sectioned report
- Include what you did / found and the concrete evidence that supports it
- Include file paths, errors, command outcomes, or other artifacts when relevant
- Do NOT return only a title or a single-line summary
- Keep it concise, but detailed enough that the main agent can act without reopening your full trace
- Choose a status that matches the outcome: use 'success' for fully completed work, 'partial' for meaningful progress with straightforward continuation, 'blocked' for external blockers that you cannot resolve autonomously, and 'failure' for invalid or unrecoverable paths
